Zydus Wellness

author.name: Tarun Arora
author.info: Chief Executive Officer, Zydus Wellness Limited ; President - CIFTI (FICCI)

postedAt.postedAgoText: 1 year ago • Edited • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

repost.content:
It’s always fun speaking to Tarun Arora! He says I “get info out of him”

I think it’s his business and brands that spark my curiosity more & more with every conversation that we have. 

Today he was candid enough to admit that Q1 was a bad one for some of his key brands, but hopeful enough to believe the remaining three quarters would make up for it. 

Obviously, we ended with a long term milestone promise. He intends to make Zydus Wellness a 5000 cr revenue in 5 years organically. Knowing their knack for acquisitions, I conclude it’s going to come in 3-4 years itself. He smiles without saying anything else. 🤣

#StockMarket #Conversations #Leadership #Consumption #FMCG #India #FMCGisLife #Interview #CNBC 

https://lnkd.in/dFcQKwkk

post_data.content:
Quick commerce is rewriting the playbook for consumer brands. It’s no longer just about delivering faster. It’s about reimagining how brands build relevance, distribute at scale, and create deeper consumer connections. As consumer journeys become non-linear and loyalty grows fragile, agility and data have become the new competitive moats. To stay ahead, brands need a differential equation mindset—balancing speed, scale, and long-term strategy in real time. The future will belong to those who can pivot fast while building deep!

https://lnkd.in/dHgWPXQt

article.title: Will Quick Commerce Set in Motion a Differential Equation Approach for Consumer Brands?
article.subtitle: brandwagon.financialexpress.com
article.linkLabel: Open article: Will Quick Commerce Set in Motion a Differential Equation Approach for Consumer Brands? by brandwagon.financialexpress.com

post_data.content:
When Complan met Horlicks !!

Did we discuss these two iconic brands? Not much, the conversation was more about the opportunities in Nutrition and perspectives from deep understanding of the emerging consumer needs, leading organization in a BANI world & making a positive difference to the employees, consumers and society at large! There is so much to learn and absorb when you have as accomplished a guest for a Fireside chat as I did couple of days back!
 
I had the privilege of hosting Sanjiv Mehta, for our leadership forum, Leadership Unplugged. He has created tremendous value for all stakeholders including over 60Bn USD for shareholders in his decade long leadership at HUL. In his new avatar as a Board member and advisor, he helps corporations like Danone, Air India and PE firm, L Catterton create a similar magic. He agreed to share a valuable day from his busy schedule for the Zydus Wellness Team who joined in for the session across countries from UK to UAE to various locations in India and 150 of us in person at Zydus Corporate Park.
 
The conversations with him were mesmerizing to say the least. He told me and the audience in the auditorium to ask anything however uncomfortable it may sound. He responded to each question with humility and erudite command over the subjects across the spectrum.
 
And of course, I was lucky enough to receive a copy of his personally signed book that just arrived on shelves, “A CEO’s Brew”.
 
Each of the things he said were gems encapsulated in insights and perspective gathered over decades of building leading businesses globally. He talked at great length about performance through people and how the two are not in conflict with each other. Further elaborating on winning teams, he talked about Growth mindset and Owners’ mindset and how these contribute to success of the organization. He urged the team to act like owners however small their responsibility may be and make a difference to what they do.
 
He elaborated on Humbition, a word that he has written about in his book. He talked about juxtaposing humility with ambition, that powers you through success without getting arrogant or losing sight of the fact that you may not know everything and still learn from others. He talked with equal dexterity about his experience navigating various disruptions across continents and timelines and converting those disruptions into opportunities that helped Unilever win against odds.
 
Mixing between personal anecdotes and organizational values, Sanjiv weaved a picture of leadership and competing that transcended businesses and geographies, that involved converging approaches across many countries in MENA to Winning in Many Indias through creating cohorts & clusters to cater to diverse expectations of consumers.
 
Session left an enduring memory in the minds of young & ambitious colleagues. Many of the values & people first philosophy resonated with the Zydus Group values.
 
I look forward to hosting you again!

postedAt.postedAgoText: 1 year ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

repost.content:
Good conversation, as always, with Tarun Arora of Zydus Wellness 

Key Takeaways

They target early teens revenue growth in FY26

Aspire to get to 17-18% margins in 2 years

25-30% CAGR for RiteBite is sustainable, grew over 40-50% in the last few months

Will grow Personal care at 2X pace of Nutrition portfolio

Modern trade & digital commerce contribute 22-23% of total business

10-10.5% of sales from online, 40% of that comes from Qcomm

Desire to cross 4 million outlets in GT over the next few years

#StockMarket #FMCGisLife #Consumption #Leadership #ManagementInterview #FMCG

post_data.content:
One of the many interesting shifts in marketing today is where value is being built. Not in how loudly a brand shows up, but in where it chooses to belong.

Women’s cricket in India has become one of those rare spaces where performance, identity, and aspiration intersect. It is no longer just a sporting property. It is a living reflection of how women are reshaping the cultural narrative around ambition and performance.

For a brand like Everyuth Naturals, which is built on everyday confidence and the idea that comfort in your own skin is foundational, this is a space that carries meaning. Its association with the Gujarat Giants reflects a choice to participate in a cultural movement, not simply a season. I believe brands earn relevance when they embed themselves in platforms that expand what people believe is possible.

Wishing the Gujarat Giants a season defined by courage, character, and competitive spirit.

This is not just an association. It is an alignment of purpose.
A women-centric brand that has always stood for everyday confidence.
A franchise that represents the bold, competitive future of women’s sport.
Together, we are backing a movement that is reshaping culture, where women own the spotlight, rewrite narratives, and inspire millions to believe bigger.
